reviews.text:
I have visited Ho Chi Minh City several times over the last 12 months and I am very disappointed  at the reception I received from the front office staff. They were most unfriendly and showed a total lack of PR.

On the day when I was supposed to check out I jokingly told the  front desk that I may be late by 10 minutes. and the immediate response from them was that I will be charged an extra day for being 10 minutes late to check out.. Such is the unfriendly attitude of the front office staff.  

I strongly urge my friends to bypass this hotel

reviews.title: Rude staff
reviews.text: When we booked my husband and 1 year old baby contacted the hotel and arranged a late checkout and were told because we had a late checkin a late checkout would be no extra cost. On arrival at the hotel we were met at the front desk by the supervisor who called us liars that they agreed to a late checkout. We wouldn't of cared so much except for the rude and arrogant way she went about it. The wifi did not work properly in the room and at one stage my husband went down to the front desk 5 times in one day after calling the front desk 3 times and no one came to rectify the problem in the end we just gave up. The previous occupant had left underwear on the window to dry and were hanging there when we went into the room. We organized a infants cot for our son and that arrived on the second day. I've stay in 5 star hotels and in noway is this a 5 star. Some customer service training wouldn't go a stray. Maybe the tourism department gives out stars to government owned hotels willy nilly. Very disappointed by the service especially the front desks arrogance.

reviews.text: If you want to be near to the airport there are other options. But there is just no other reason to go to that hotel. It starts with the strange smell in the lobby which is definitely unique but not nice. Very soon you will figure out that this place is famous among a special class and age of Vietnamese gentlemen who love to celebrate their success in this hotel and its bar until they have to be carried back home. Do not go to the SPA, it might have been bad luck that I got an unprofessional therapist but once you want to pay you will be informed that the SPA is not belonging to the Hotel and has to be paid separately and you will also be informed that there are high expectations in how much tip you are giving - very pushy. In case you go to the GYM, be prepared to be alone there. Finally; if you are asking for a coffee latte at breakfast it takes some time for one of the staff to bring it from the coffee shop nearby... Notice: I'm for business in Vietnam monthly since 7 years

reviews.title: 5 Star once maybe, but not anymore
reviews.text:
This hotel has a roach problem which is probably why its being discounted the way it is. We saw 5 within the first 10 minutes of being there and we were staying on the 11th floor - no where near food or kitchens.

The rooms are very large and I'm sure would have been considered as luxurious 20 years ago, but I think nothing has been done with them since. The carpets are dirty and the bathrooms outdated - the only shower was an over the bath set-up with a maximum showerhead height of about 4ft 6in - something of a struggle.

Location is good for the airport, but the taxi drivers don't like it - we had one that pretended to get lost on his way to the hotel just to bump up his fare. There is nowhere within walking distance where you can get food or drink outside of the hotels so taxis are a must - although they are plentiful and cheap so not a huge issue - just be aware.
reviews.positives: Location - 10 minutes from the airport, Price - heavily discounted, bed - large and clean

reviews.title: Could Have Been Nice
reviews.text: Hello, we stayed a couple of nights. Our first impression when arrived at the hotel & lobby was a wow, this hotel is very nice. Then, we were shown our room (deluxe double twin beds) and it was below standard. 1) Room was musty with very old carpet. 2) The two twin beds actually rolled when changing position while sitting on the bed. 3) Worst of all were the mattresses were literally pushing up when sitting or laying on the bed. We spent two miserable nights there. I am sure management knows about this issues in this and some other rooms (per reviews) but has not made the changes. Customers should not have to sleep on such a terrible mattress while paying the price we paid. Please update your rooms then you can consider the hotel a 4 star.

reviews.title: Overall is ok except for the staff
reviews.text:
Generally speaking things went quite well. When I first checked in, the receptionist was friendly and let me check in early before 12am since the room was available. However, when I asked for 2 breakfast vouchers she rejected to give me with the reason that I only checked in with one person (myself), while my room is Double Deluxe and I am entitled for 2 vouchers. What if I wanted to invite my friend over for breakfast?! Come on, I already had to stay there alone and now you're implying that I gotta eat alone as well? Plus, the baggage porter was not very friendly. 
I only stayed there for 1 day, the next morning after checking out I went to have a coffee at the Blue Lounge  and was disappointed at the attitude of the staff there (not sure if they also belong to hotel's staff as it's part of hotel's restaurants & cafes). I was in a bit of a hurry to go to the airport but waited for 5-10mins only to know that no one is gonna come and ask for my order, unless I gotta go and ask myself.

reviews.title: Slightly disappinting 5 Star which in fact is 3 Star max!
reviews.text: I've booked the hotel because it claims to be a 5 Star and it's in convenient distance to the airport. The hotel is very old fashion, worn out and presents a kind of Sovjet Union style from the late 1960ees. The staff is friendly but not really supportive. They seem more busy with their smart phones than with their customers. We also had a real drama when my gf and me want to check in they asked for the marriage certificate. Hello guys, is this North Corea or what? At least they agree that we could stay together by signing a paper which doesn't mean anything else that she isn't a whore and I'm not her pimp! The room was ok and clean but old fashion as well. The breakfast buffet was less than average, I have eaten better in Cambodian hostels! One thing is for sure; this hotel never ever again!

reviews.text: The hotel feels old but has been well maintained. There is a bathtub, but visually I did not feel inclined to soak in it. The bathtub is quite high and dangerous. The shower water flows outside. The room is spacious and the bed was comfortable to sleep in. There were many choices for breakfast and it was very good. There was a supermarket, convenience store, and café across the street, but crossing was quite difficult. I ran out of water and asked if I could buy some inside the hotel, and was directed to a convenience store right in front of me. However, I hesitated to cross because it seemed scary, and was about to give up when I was asked if I wanted them to bring me some water. I would have appreciated that service when I mentioned I didn't have water. Should I have said I was overseas? Still, it deserves a ★5. There were a few similar instances. The transportation is close to the airport. The only way to get to tourist spots was via Grab, but the fare was cheap so it didn't bother me much.

reviews.negatives:
I did notice some problems this time that I think should be addressed
1. I was assigned a floor with Chinese guests that talked very loud, almost shouting. Their voices spread through the hall and were disturbing at night when I wanted to catch a sleep. Front desk associates may want to be careful when assigning guests from different nationalities, cultures and values.
2. The non-smoking room that I was assigned, room 911, had scent of cigarettes. The cup was used as an ashtray, not cleaned properly. Housekeeping team may need retraining.
3. I was very happy to see that the hotel has restarted a Chinese restaurant on the 2nd floor. They had an excellent Cantonese restaurant before, but has closed, moved to district 7. Now the hotel has a new restaurant with the same chef as before. Food is great! The only improvement needed is the staffs language skills and CS manners. They do not speak English or Chinese. Difficult to address customers' needs and requests. Hope the hotel management will pay attention on their training and skill development.

reviews.title: Average and Clean
reviews.text: Even though the hotel rates itself as a 5-star, it's really is a 3.5 star.  We stayed 1 night knowing that we have a flight to catch the next morning.  Our rom was a suite room; it was large and clean.  The bathroom has a full-sized bath tub.  The swimming pool was a lap-sized pool, and  it wasn't over-chlorinated.  The breakfast spread was a little disappointing; I guess you can call it average.  I didn't see any restaurants nearby.  If you are looking for a large room to stay near the airport for a day, then this hotel is an option.

reviews.title: Good value. Safe. Clean.
reviews.positives: We stayed at this hotel on a local's recommendation, and we had a wonderful stay. My uncle dropped his wallet in the lobby and they made the effort to return it to him after doing due diligence to make sure he is the rightful owner. A big thank you to Hoa and Linh.  Breakfast was great, and there are a few good eateries within the hotel. Rooms and corridors were clean. There is a nice sunny pool on the 4th floor and a gym in the next building just 1min walk away. There's a convenience store across the road. Generally happy, and will stay here again. Another thing we like abt the hotel is its proximity to the airport.  Less than 10min car ride. Easy to call a Grab (local uber) to anywhere. Never have to wait for more than 5min.
reviews.negatives: I wish the steam room and sauna at the pool men change room were working. That would have been nice. We couldn't get it working when we were there.

We also had a very good seafood buffet on Friday evening for only 500,000VND per person. They also provided airport transfer for 2 people: from the airport - 400,000VND, and to the airport next day - 300,000VND, total 700,000VND. It might be a bit more expensive than a cab, but you will avoid any scam, especially if you don't have local SIM card yet. Accept Visa and Mastercard.

reviews.title: Sketchy WiFi, disappointing 5 Star.
reviews.text:
Here is a list of pros and cons.

Pros:-
1. Clean.
2. Big Rooms.
3. Relatively affordable for a "5" Star, even though its only a local standard one.
4. Near to Airport.
5. Warm and friendly front desk and housekeeping staff.
6. Big bathroom.
7. Big pool.
8. Mini Golf range.
9. Good restaurants in the premises.

Cons:-
1. Lousy WiFi/Internet, could not get on the internet even when connected.
2. Bad attitudes amongst certain staff & cooks at the breakfast dining restaurant.
3. Old & dated interior design, even though hotel was officially opened in 2012.
4. Lack of amenities in the immediate area, after the tearing down of Big C which was located right beside it. (no shopping, fast food, etc)
5. Currently having 2 constructions going on in its immediate vicinity, which is running 24/7. One on the hotel's South side, and the other on the West Side. Loud sudden noises can be heard in the middle of the night, awakening me several time over the course of my week long stay there.
6. No staff to prevent Buses from clogging up the front entrance driveway. 
7. In my opinion, there seems to be a lack of staff for the hotel of this size and compound area.


I will not recommend staying in this hotel if you are a light sleeper, as the current construction going on will definitely be a deal breaker.

If room size/amenities is a concern, there are alternatives near to the airport, that are newer and in lack of a better word modern.

Although the buffet didn't have a huge diversity, the food in buffet was diverse enough to me and delicious  which made me feel like that they do care more about quality than quantity.

Their was some building work going on till late next door and despite being on 12th floor we could still hear it. I don't think the window in our room shut properly. 
In terms of Location it's really near the airport so very convenient for quick stopover

reviews.title: Amazing breakfast!
reviews.positives: This is our "go to" hotel in Ho Chi Minh City. The rooms are clean and roomy, the lobby is beautifully decorated, the pool is clean and refreshing, the staff is polite and very helpful. But the best reason to keep returning is the breakfast! It is amazing with so many choices, the biggest breakfast dining experience we've ever had. The hotel is also close to the airport - 5 to 10 minutes depending on traffic. We have stayed at this hotel several times and highly recommend it.

The hotel can upgrade its tv and aircon system then it should be outstanding .

reviews.title: Comfortable and new airport hotel

reviews.text: The location is close the airport and easy to netvigating to the customer outside the city (get rid of traffic jam). The hotel provide the good restaurant and meal. But if you want to walk around, this is nothing to see, better stay in the hotel and enjoy the internal facilities.

reviews.title: A nice stay
reviews.text: We stayed here for only one night. I tell you tho, the breakfast was amazing. The value for money of the breakfast is so great. The breakfast alone is worth the visit. They have everything I wanted to eat and some more. The room was very spacious and it was quite cheap for that price. The bath room was huge and clean. It's very quiet and peaceful. The location of the hotel is great for those who want to fly out the next day. It's very close to the air port. I guess it depends on where you want to stay, in the city center or near the air port. I like the city a lot but if you need to fly out the next day and want to avoid the crazy traffic, this is a good hotel to stay in.

reviews.title: Very nice hotel, convenient to airport

reviews.title: Maybe a 4.5* with a push
reviews.text:
Large grounds with a sister 3 star representing good value. Not close to the city center which with traffic density makes a long trip. Breakfast great, Chinese restaurant first rate and not over the top.
A little TLC and attention to detail ( clean off walls in Great Bear restaurant - maybe bear prints? ) would see this hotel close to 5*. Staff are great.
Close to airport so easy even with Saigons traffic. Big C supermarket very close. Happy to return

reviews.title: Good
reviews.text:
Whilst overall I enjoyed and would return to this hotel I do not think it reaches a 5* bar, maybe with a push a 4.5. It needs some extra care
Negatives are not major but notable : -
> breakfast dining area walls have marks , spillages and splashes alongside the tables( I breakfasted 12 days ) 
> On the 13th floor ( Fl 12A ) the disco music was pumping
The location is close only to the airport. Given the traffic in Saigon you need to allow for this.
On the plus side :-
> Staff are very good
> The hotels are in large grounds so you are able to walk if wish
> Their is a sister 3* next door which is reasonable
> Chinese restaurant on 2nd floor is very good and reasonable price
> Rooms of a good standard and bathroom OK although not 5*
> Pool is good and gym is adequate/ good
> Taxis are very cheap in Saigon
> Big C supermarket 5 minutes walk

reviews.title: Great looking hotel, Close to the airport
reviews.text: We stayed at this hotel for two nights before flying back home. We chose the hotel because it was 5 stars and close to the airport, suitable for our early flight out. As we got to the hotel, very impressed with how nice it looked and hotel staff was nice, however we were very soon disappointed with our room, the carpet was very dirty with alot of dark spots. Swimming pool was nice with views, but was crowded when we were there. I enjoyed the sauna & steam room inside the Gent bathroom at the pool. Breakfast was really good, but staff are not very talkative. They were nice & polite, but a few friendly small chat would definitely make a difference in my opinion. Door staff is not always there,  so we always had to walk out of the front door for about 20-50 meters to the gate where all the taxi drivers were, gave all of them the signal that we wanted their service. Thought all of that could have been organised.
reviews.positives: Location close to the airport, Nice looking hotel and very good breakfast
